author: mark
text:
git_ensure_user on every command path

Move git_ensure_user() into Store::discover() so every command path
(create, claim, review, close, sync, dep, link, drop, update) seeds
a default user.email/user.name before any commit. Previously this
only ran inside Store::init(), so a fresh system whose local repo
identity was missing or got cleared would hit "Author identity
unknown" on the first git commit and fail (or hang in CI hooks
that swallowed stderr).

Worktrees share the main repo's config, so one ensure_user call on
the main root covers the state worktree and feature worktrees too.

Adds a fresh-install regression test that runs bl init/create/claim/
update/review in a temp repo with HOME pointing at an empty dir and
GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null, then explicitly clears the local
identity after init to prove discover() re-seeds it. Verified: the
new test fails on the prior code with "Author identity unknown"
and passes with the fix.
